name: Santes Creus Monastery and Coll de les Ventoses Loop
name_original: Paisajes del Baix Penedès – circular desde el Vendrell
description: Experience a difficult 48.0-mile road cycling route through the diverse landscapes of Baix Penedès, starting from El Vendrell.
region_intro: Road cycling around El Vendrell offers a diverse landscape on the Costa Daurada, suitable for various skill levels. The region features coastal routes with Mediterranean views, flat plains through vineyards in the Penedès wine region, and challenging climbs in the Montmell Mountains. Terrain varies from paved roads to sections alternating with dirt roads, providing a range of cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around El Vendrell The most popular road cycling route is Santes Creus Monastery and Coll de les Ventoses Loop, a 48.0 miles (77.2 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 42 minutes to complete. This route includes significant elevation gain and passes by the Santes Creus Monastery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Scenic Country Road – Ermita de Clarà loop from Sant Vicenç de Calders, a moderate 35.3 miles (56.8 km) path. This route offers scenic country roads and passes by the Ermita de Clarà. Local road cyclists also love the Coll de les Ventoses Climb – Coll de les Ventoses loop from el Vendrell, a 47.1 miles (75.9 km) trail leading through the Montmell Mountains, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Road cycling around El Vendrell is defined by coastal paths, Penedès vineyards, and climbs in the Montmell Mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to routes with significant elevation changes. The routes in El Vendrell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 4,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore El Vendrell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in El Vendrell?
A: There are over 70 road cycling routes around El Vendrell, offering a diverse range of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, with over 4,000 road cyclists using them.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around El Vendrell?
A: El Vendrell offers a varied landscape for road cycling. You can find picturesque coastal routes with Mediterranean views, flat plains that wind through the vineyards of the Penedès wine region, and more challenging climbs in the Montmell Mountains. While primarily paved, some routes in the Baix Penedès plain might alternate with dirt sections.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in El Vendrell?
A: Yes, El Vendrell provides several challenging routes, particularly in the Montmell Mountains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962380239/santes-creus-monastery-and-coll-de-les-ventoses-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santes Creus Monastery and Coll de les Ventoses Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 48.0-mile (77.2 km) route with significant elevation gain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24866327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de les Ventoses Climb – Coll de les Ventoses loop from el Vendrell&lt;/a&gt;, which is 47.1 miles (75.9 km) and also features considerable climbing.

Q: Are there easier or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in El Vendrell?
A: Yes, El Vendrell has options suitable for beginners and those looking for easier rides. There are 10 easy routes available, often found along the coast or in the flatter sections of the Baix Penedès plain. These routes provide a great way to enjoy the scenery without significant climbs.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through the Penedès wine region?
A: Absolutely. The Penedès wine region is a prominent feature of road cycling around El Vendrell. Routes like the 'Landscapes of the Penedès Region' (mentioned in region research) take you through vineyards and offer a blend of cultural and natural beauty. The Baix Penedès plain also features flat routes through this area.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by notable landmarks. You can cycle along the coast with Mediterranean vistas, through the vineyards of the Penedès, or past the Foix Reservoir, which offers spectacular views, especially of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/440250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/436998" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torredembarra Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1415235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Giralda of Arboç&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in El Vendrell?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in El Vendrell are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962380239/santes-creus-monastery-and-coll-de-les-ventoses-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santes Creus Monastery and Coll de les Ventoses Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19871304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Country Road – Ermita de Clarà loop from Sant Vicenç de Calders&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical distance of road cycling routes in the area?
A: The road cycling routes around El Vendrell vary significantly in distance. Many popular routes range from approximately 35 miles (56 km) to 48 miles (77 km), such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19871304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Country Road – Ermita de Clarà loop&lt;/a&gt; at 35.3 miles (56.8 km) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962380239/santes-creus-monastery-and-coll-de-les-ventoses-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santes Creus Monastery and Coll de les Ventoses Loop&lt;/a&gt; at 48.0 miles (77.2 km).

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in El Vendrell?
A: El Vendrell, being on the Costa Daurada, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with mild temperatures and less intense sun, perfect for enjoying both coastal and inland routes. Summer can be hot, especially for mountain climbs, but coastal rides might still be enjoyable.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in El Vendrell?
A: While specific family-friendly road cycling routes are not detailed, the region's diverse terrain includes easier, flatter sections, particularly along the coast or in the Baix Penedès plain. These areas, often with paved surfaces, could be suitable for families looking for a more relaxed ride. The 'Hike from Coma-ruga to Torredembarra' (mentioned in region research) is largely paved and suitable for all skill levels, suggesting similar road cycling options exist.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the road cycling experience in El Vendrell?
A: The road cycling experience in El Vendrell is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscape, which offers everything from scenic coastal paths to challenging mountain climbs and beautiful vineyard routes.

Q: Are there routes that offer coastal views?
A: Yes, El Vendrell is located on the Costa Daurada, and many road cycling routes feature stunning coastal sections. These routes provide picturesque views of the Mediterranean Sea, offering a refreshing experience, especially during milder weather. The 'Hike from Coma-ruga to Torredembarra' (mentioned in region research) is an example of a route that offers scenic coastal views.

Q: Can I incorporate historical sites into my road cycling tour?
A: Yes, you can certainly combine road cycling with visits to historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962380239/santes-creus-monastery-and-coll-de-les-ventoses-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santes Creus Monastery and Coll de les Ventoses Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by the Santes Creus Monastery. Additionally, routes around the Foix Reservoir offer views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/440250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a significant historical landmark.
tips: Very well preserved castle, located on the top of a small hill, on the outskirts of the municipality of Santa Oliva. The architectural complex, made up of two towers, was initially built to defend against Muslim rule.
This section coincides with a large part of the climb to Coll de les Ventoses or Alt de la Talaia. It is a beautiful and highly recommended section.

The climb to Les Ventoses is one of the most popular in the area, with its seven kilometers and a very constant average gradient of 4.5%, which never goes beyond 6%. It is always frequented by cyclists and has a low level of road traffic. The road is perfectly paved and as it goes up, it allows you to contemplate a beautiful panoramic view of the Penedès plain and even the Mediterranean Sea, thanks to its numerous horseshoe curves.

The Monastery of Santa María de Santes Creus is a Cistercian abbey built in the 12th century. The grandeur of the monastery is due to the donations received from the nobility who wanted to be buried here.

It is a magnificent Modernist-style building located in the municipality of Montferri, in the Alt Camp region. Its architect, Josep Maria Jujol, took the Montserrat mountains as inspiration for his great work.
The church is a neo-romantic composition from the 19th century. It was restaured in 1941 by the famous modernist architect Josep Maria Jujol.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: This circular route starts and ends in El Vendrell, the capital of Baix Penedès. You'll find various public parking options within the town, suitable for cyclists looking to begin their ride.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult, designed for experienced road cyclists with very good fitness. It includes significant elevation gains, such as the challenging Coll de les Ventoses Climb, which is 7.7 kilometers long with a 4.5% gradient.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse experience, transitioning from coastal areas near El Vendrell through flat plains with extensive vineyards and olive groves, characteristic of the Penedès wine region. As you move inland, you'll encounter forested hills and well-paved roads, making it ideal for road bikes.

faqs.question: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll pass by several interesting sites, including the historic Castillo de Santa Oliva, the scenic Coll de les Ventoses, and significant religious buildings like the Royal Monastery of Santes Creus and the Montserrat Sanctuary, Montferri. There are also beautiful views, such as the View of Santa Magdalena de Bonastre.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Baix Penedès region is pleasant for cycling for much of the year. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) generally offer the most comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery, with vineyards in full bloom or harvest. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. You'll briefly encounter parts of the CIMA TA08 Ventoses (La Talaia), Penedès 360 Mountain Bike (ruta 5 dies), and the Sender de Santes Creus, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Baix Penedès region?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or generally within the Baix Penedès region. You are free to enjoy the public roads and trails.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' classification and significant elevation gain, this specific route is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. However, the broader El Vendrell area offers many other circular routes with varying difficulty levels that might be more suitable for less experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog along on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome in many outdoor areas, this is a road cycling route primarily on paved surfaces and public roads. It's not typically suitable for cycling with dogs, especially given the distance and difficulty. If you plan to stop at any establishments, check their individual pet policies.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging ride?
faqs.answer: For this difficult racebike route, ensure you bring plenty of water and nutrition, as well as essential bike repair tools and a first-aid kit. Given the varied terrain and potential for sun exposure, appropriate cycling attire, sunscreen, and a helmet are crucial. Consider a GPS device for navigation.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for food and drink stops along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through various towns and villages, including El Vendrell itself, there are opportunities to find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. The Penedès region is also famous for its wines, so you might find wineries offering tastings, though check opening times in advance.

waypoints.name: View of Santa Magdalena de Bonastre
